data.title: Starbucks China names first chief growth officer amid stiff competition - report
data.description:
U.S. coffee giant Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) appointed its first chief growth officer for China operations amid declining sales in its second-largest market after the U.S., Reuters reported, citing Chinese media outlet.

Tony Yang, formerly a partner and president at the digital marketing agency Tezign, will take on this newly created role, the report said.

As the chief growth officer of Starbucks (SBUX) China, Tony Yang will have several key responsibilities aimed at driving growth and enhancing the company's competitive edge in a challenging market.

The move comes amid restructuring; Molly Liu was named the sole CEO of Starbucks (SBUX) China a few months back, while Belinda Wong transitioned to the role of chairwoman after previously sharing CEO responsibilities.

The company faced declining sales, reporting a 14% drop in comparable store sales for the fiscal year ending September 2024, with both average prices and transaction volumes decreasing significantly.

Starbucks (SBUX) operates over 7,500 stores in China and faces intense competition from local brands such as Luckin Coffee (OTCPK:LKNCY) and Cotti Coffee.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher prices $8.5B stock offering
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) has priced an offering of $8.5B of shares of its common stock at $280.00 per share.
  • Gallagher also granted the underwriters in the offering a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4,553,571 shares at the same price.
  • Net proceeds of this offering will be used to fund a portion of the cash consideration payable in connection with the company’s proposed acquisition of AssuredPartners and any remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • In addition to the net proceeds from this offering, Gallagher expects to use available cash and available borrowings under its revolving credit facility or other borrowings to fund the purchase price for the transaction.

data.title: Kingfisher Metals looks to raise C$1.25M via private placement
data.description:
• Kingfisher Metals (OTCQB:KGFMF) said it is looking to raise C$1.25M through a non-brokered private placement of units at C$0.165 apiece.
  • Each unit will have one share and one half of one transferable common share purchase warrant.
  • The firm added each whole warrant will be exercisable to acquire one additional common share of firm for 24 months from the closing date of the offering at an exercise price of C$0.30.
  • The net proceeds are expected to be used for continuing to expand Kingfisher's business, including the acquisition of the Ball Creek West project from P2 Gold, and general working capital purposes.

data.title: Nokia extends partnership with Iliad to enhance 4G and 5G networks in France and Italy
data.description:
• Nokia (NYSE:NOK) and French telecom giant Iliad Group have decided to expand their collaboration that began in 2010 via a new multi-year contract extension to support the operator’s 3G, 4G, and 5G mobile network services across France and Italy.
  • The deal not only encompasses their operations in France and Italy but also extends to the strategic regions of the French Caribbean and the Indian Ocean Islands.
  • Through this multi-year contract extension, Nokia will provide solutions from its AirScale portfolio, including baseband, Massive MIMO radios, Remote Radio Head products, and Core solutions, enabling Iliad to deploy new network services securely in multi-cloud environments.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ORCL, AI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped in Tuesday's premarket session as investors awaited the U.S. consumer price index report, scheduled for release on Wednesday. This report could play a key role in shaping the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rates during its December 17-18 meeting.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • C3.ai (AI) shares gained over 7% after the company raised its full-year guidance following stronger-than-expected FQ2 results, driven by robust artificial intelligence-related spending. The company now expects FY2025 revenue to range from $378M to $398M, up from the prior forecast of $370M to $395M, surpassing the consensus of $382.6M. For Q3, C3.ai anticipates revenue between $95.5M and $100.5M, with the midpoint of $98M slightly exceeding the consensus of $97.5M.
  • Vail Resorts (MTN) shares rose nearly 5% after surpassing consensus expectations in its FQ1 results and raising its FY2025 outlook. The company increased its net income guidance for the fiscal year ending July 31, 2025, to a range of $240M to $316M, up from the prior forecast of $224M to $300M, largely driven by the sale of real estate. This revised guidance assumes stable economic conditions, normal weather for the 2024/2025 North American and European ski seasons, the 2025 Australian ski season, and consistent foreign exchange rates.

Biggest stock losers

  • Oracle (ORCL) shares slumped more than 8% after reporting lower-than-expected Q2 results. For the current quarter, the IT giant forecasts revenue growth of 7% to 9%, below the consensus estimate of 10.33%, and expects adjusted earnings of $1.50 to $1.54 per share, matching the consensus of $1.50.

data.title: Getlink reports 4% decline in shuttle traffic in November
data.description:
• Getlink (OTCPK:GRPTY) Tuesday said that LeShuttle Freight carried 104,436 trucks in November, down 4% compared to a year ago and down 2.24% sequentially.
  • Since 1 January, more than 1 million trucks have crossed the Channel on board the Shuttles.
  • LeShuttle carried 112,478 passenger vehicles in November, up 2% compared to November 2023.
  • Since 1 January, more than 2 million passenger vehicles have been transported.
  • The December traffic figures will be published on Thursday, January 09, pre-market.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's November revenue surges 34%
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (NYSE:TSM) revenue for November soared 34% year-over-year to about NT$276.06B.

However, revenue for November declined 12.2% compared to October, when the company generated NT$314.24B.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) — saw revenue from January through November 2024 amount to NT$2.616T, growing 31.8% compared to the same period in 2023.

TSM is potentially seen as a bellwether for the build-out of AI data centers. Generative AI services have taken the world by storm since the launch of OpenAI's ChatGPT in 2022. Globally, companies have launched their own large language models, or LLMs, which can provide services such as content, image, video, and voice generation, to name a few. These LLMs require AI chips to process large amounts of data.

During TSM's third-quarter earnings call in October, Chairman and CEO C.C. Wei had said that the AI demand was real and that it was just the beginning.

Year-to-date, the Taiwan-based company's stock has surged about 91%.

TSM -1.32% to $196.54 premarket on Dec. 10.

data.title: MAIA Biotechnology announces $950,000 private placement
data.description:
MAIA Biotechnology (NYSE:MAIA) entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of an aggregate of 507,364 shares at $1.872 per share, in a private placement to accredited investors and certain company directors.

Each share is being offered together with a warrant to purchase one share at an exercise price of $2.08 per share.The warrants are exercisable commencing six months following issuance and have a term of five years from the initial exercise date.

The securities being sold to the company director participating in the offering are being issued pursuant to the company’s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan.

The private placement is expected to close on or about December 11, 2024.

The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $950,000.

Proceeds will be used to fund manufacturing of THIO for Phase II clinical trials and for working capital.

The stock price dropped 3.2% on Monday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: European markets dip as traders await U.S. inflation figures
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.42%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.15% Germany's inflation rises to 2.2% in November as expected

France (CAC:IND) -0.49%

Industrial production in Slovakia rose by 1.3% Y/Y in October.

Industrial production in Austria fell by 1.3% Y/Y in October.

The annual inflation rate in Moldova ticked up to 5.4% in November.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic stood at 2.8% in November.

Producer prices in Lithuania dropped by 1.8% Y/Y in November. Lithuania's trade deficit widened to €0.462B in October.

The annual inflation rate in Hungary increased to 3.7% in November.

The annual inflation rate in Denmark stood at 1.6% in November.

Romania's trade deficit increased to €3.72B in October.

Industrial production in Sweden dropped by 2% Y/Y in October.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.24% lower, snapping an eight-day winning streak. Investor sentiment was dampened by disappointing trade data from China. Traders refrain from making big bets ahead of the US inflation data due tomorrow and the ECB's monetary policy decision on Thursday.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on economic front.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield up 1 basis points to 4.21%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.13%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.29%.

data.title: McDonald's review bombed on Google after UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect's arrest
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) restaurants in Altoona, Pennsylvania were review bombed online after a suspect was arrested in connection with the shooting of a UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) executive, prompting Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) to remove the negative reviews.

UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was fatally shot last Wednesday, triggering a nationwide manhunt for the shooter. Luigi Mangione was arrested at a McDonald's (MCD) restaurant in Altoona on Monday after an employee tipped off police. Mangione has been charged with one count of second-degree murder and four other charges.

Thompson's murder unleashed social media rage against U.S. health insurers, with many users showing no sympathy for the executive's death and instead slamming companies for denying claims or care.

Following Mangione's arrest, negative and one-star ratings were posted for around three McDonald's (MCD) locations in or around Altoona, complaining about "snitches."

"This location has rats in the kitchen that will make you sick and your insurance isn't going to cover it," one user posted.

"These reviews violate our policies and have been removed," a Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. According to its policies, reviews must be based on real experiences with a location.

The hostel where Mangione stayed in New York also saw similar negative reviews online, after the NYPD circulated the suspect's photos taken at the hostel's front desk.

Related stories

  • UnitedHealthcare CEO's Death Raises Tough Questions
  • Man questioned in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO
  • UnitedHealth shooting weighs on health insurance stocks
  • UnitedHealthcare CEO killed in 'premeditated' shooting
